author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
āmi aparādhi⋅jana, sadā daṇḍya, durlakṣaṇa,
sahasra sahasra doṣe doṣī
bhīma bhavārṇavodare, patita viṣama ghore,
gatihīna gati⋅abhilāṣī

hari! tava pādadvaye, śaraṇa loinu bhaye,
kṛpā kari’ kara ātmasātha
tomāra pratijñā ei, śaraṇa loibe jei,
tumi tā’ra rakṣākartā nātha

pratijñāte kari’ bhara, o mādhava prāṇeśvara!
śaraṇa loila ei dāsa
e bhaktivinoda gāya, tomāra se rāṅgāpāya,
deha dāse sevāya vilāsa
en_translation:
O Hari! I am a sinful person, always punishable, possessing bad qualities, guilty of thousands and thousands of faults. In the terrible ocean of material existence, I have fallen into a dreadful, difficult situation, without shelter, yet desiring to find shelter.

Hari! At Your two feet, I take shelter with fear; please mercifully accept me as Your own. This is Your promise: whoever takes Your shelter, You are their protector, my Lord.

Relying on that promise, O Mādhava, Lord of my life! This servant has taken shelter. Bhaktivinoda sings: at Your reddish lotus feet, please grant this servant the joy of service.
